message:
fix(doctor): add worktree-gitdir-valid check for broken .repo.git references

Adds a doctor check that validates worktree .git files reference existing
gitdir paths. Detects the scenario where a rig's .repo.git is missing or
a worktree entry doesn't exist, causing git operations to fail with
"fatal: not a git repository". The check scans refinery/rig, polecats,
and witness/rig across all rigs and supports --fix to re-create broken
worktrees from .repo.git when possible.

Fixes: gt-fmnml

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -55,6 +55,7 @@ Clone divergence checks:
   - persistent-role-branches Detect crew/witness/refinery not on main
   - clone-divergence         Detect clones significantly behind origin/main
   - default-branch-all-rigs  Verify default_branch exists on remote for all rigs
+  - worktree-gitdir-valid    Verify worktree .git files reference existing paths (fixable)
 
 Crew workspace checks:
   - crew-state               Validate crew worker state.json files (fixable)
@@ -217,6 +218,9 @@ func runDoctor(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
 	d.Register(doctor.NewDoltMetadataCheck())
 	d.Register(doctor.NewDoltServerReachableCheck())
 
+	// Worktree gitdir validity (runs across all rigs, or specific rig with --rig)
+	d.Register(doctor.NewWorktreeGitdirCheck())
+
 	// Rig-specific checks (only when --rig is specified)
 	if doctorRig != "" {
 		d.RegisterAll(doctor.RigChecks()...)
